title: 96 well spin column TNA extraction from plants - CTAB method
author: James JN Kitson
materials: Borosilicate glass beaker or Duran bottle

steps.title: Source Steel Beads
steps.instruction: Source steel beads (ball bearings) for tissue grinding (Tungsten beads are not usually necessary). We use hardened 3mm carbon steel or stainless steel bearings from simplybearings.co.uk . This protocol requires three beads per sample tube.

steps.title: Grind Plant Material
steps.instruction: Place tubes while still frozen in tissue homogeniser and grind the plant material in a tissue homogeniser until homogenised. We use a Geno/Grinder 2010 at full speed (1750 RPM) for 4-5 minutes in 2 min batches using tube holders stored at the same temperature as the grinding tubes but different machines or even tube sizes and sample volumes will require separate optimised settings.

steps.title: Add CTAB Lysis Buffer & Mix
steps.instruction: To the plant material, add freshly-prepared CTAB lysis buffer (see below for volumes) , and place in a tube shaker, vortex mixer or tissue homogeniser (at low speed) to mix. For dried material add add 700 μl of CTAB lysis buffer as some will be absorbed by the dry material. For fresh material you only need 500 μl. Ensure that the powdered plant material is thoroughly mixed with the CTAB lysis buffer so that the TNA can dissolve into the supernatant.

steps.title: Centrifuge to Pellet Debris
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 5 minutes to pellet plant debris. This step is very important. Plant lysates tend to contain suspended fine particulates. These can block the spin column so it is best to remove as much as possible.

steps.title: Clean and Bake Steel Beads
steps.instruction: Beads are usually shipped coated in manufacturing oil (especially the carbon steel beads). To remove this, place beads in a borosilicate glass beaker or Duran bottle with the pouring lip and lid removed then bake for at least 12 hours at 250 o C. Figure 1: Depending on baking time, carbon steel beads will change colour, this is normal.

steps.title: Prepare CTAB Lysis Buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare CTAB lysis buffer : A B C 1 Reagent Required cencentration in buffer Amount per litre 2 CTAB 2% 20 g 3 Polyvinyl pyrrolidone 2% 20 g 4 Tris-HCl 10 mM 10 ml (of 1M stock solution) 5 EDTA 20 mM 40 ml (of 0.5M stock solution 6 Ammonium chloride 1.3M 69.54 g

steps.title: Incubate Samples in CTAB Lysis Buffer
steps.instruction: Incubate lysed samples in CTAB lysis buffer. 55 °C, for 60 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare Sample Tubes with Beads
steps.instruction: Prepare 5 ml preparation tubes ( e.g. Starlab #E1450-1100 ) containing three 3 mm hardened steel beads in batches of 90 tubes.

steps.title: Add Plant Material to Tubes
steps.instruction: Using forceps (either disposable forceps or ethanol and flame steel forceps between samples), place ~ 50 mg of dried plant material (100 mg if using fresh) into the pre-prepared collection tubes containing the hardened steel ball bearings. Be sure to label the tube with a sample number and any additional detail necessary for the study. Alternatively barcode your tubes with preprinted labels and use data collection software such as epicollect5 to record sample IDs and metadata.

steps.title: Freeze Samples
steps.instruction: Place all sample tubes in zip-lock bags in batches of 90 (to allow for two extraction negatives, a DNA negative and three positive controls). Freeze at -80 o C until ready to proceed with the extraction.

steps.title: Transfer Supernatant to Filter Plate
steps.instruction: Transfer 350 μl of supernatant to a new 96 well filter plate ( NBS biologicals #SD5006 ) placed on top of a 1-2 ml deep well plate. Seal the filter plate with a breathable plate seal ( Starlab #E2796-3005 ) Add 350 μl of CTAB buffer to two of your empty wells designated as extraction negatives.

steps.title: Add Isopropanol & Vortex
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl of 100% Isopropanol to each sample and vortex to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge to Pellet Debris (Repeat)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 5 minutes to pellet plant debris. This step is very important. Plant lysates tend to contain suspended fine particulates. These can block the spin column so it is best to remove as much as possible.

steps.title: Transfer Supernatant to Spin Column
steps.instruction: For each sample, add as much as possible* of the supernatant from step 14 to a well in a 96-well silica membrane spin column ( we use SD5007 from NBS Biologicals ) sitting on top of a 1-2 ml deep well plate and cover with a breathable seal . * without disturbing any sediment.

steps.title: Wash Spin Column with Ethanol
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through or change waste receiver and add 500 μl of 70% Ethanol. Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Repeat Washes & Dry Column
steps.instruction: Repeat step 15 two more times for a total of three washes. After the last wash spin for 10 minutes to dry the spin column.

steps.title: Elute TNA
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a new 0.5 ml deep well plate and add 50-100 μl of water or 10mM Tris-HCl. Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature and centrifuge at 1500 x g for 1 minute. The flow-through now contains the extracted TNA.

steps.title: Final Centrifugation & Storage
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 1 minute to filter and further homogenise the plant material. Sample can be stored at -20 o C until ready to proceed.

steps.title: Final Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g for 5 minutes.
